content:
- Read prior progress, refreshed Ralph task summaries, and inspected chooser rules plus task inventory.
- Confirmed the repo is a Rust workspace (`Cargo.toml`, `crates/`, `Dockerfile`, `Makefile`) and that `.ralph/current_tasks.md` remains empty because all 40 top-level task files are already represented in `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` with passing status.
- Ran `make check`, `make test`, and `make lint`; all exited cleanly. No unblocked failing bug tasks, no unblocked failing non-bug tasks, and no meta-task entries remain selectable. Next step after this context exits is none: the repo is in the Ralph stop condition for now, so `.ralph/STOP` is being touched.

- Refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, then checked the chooser rules and active task files.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks, no explicit `<priority>` or `<blocked_by>` tags among the active stories, and the current repo architecture is a Rust workspace with `runner` still carrying source/verify responsibilities plus a separate `source-bootstrap` crate and vendored Go MOLT source.
- Chose `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/01-task-remove-runner-source-cockroach-access-and-config.md` as the next task because it is the most direct boundary-hardening step for the current architecture and it cleanly precedes the later verify-image, SQL-emitter, and runner-image split stories.

- Read Ralph startup context: previous progress log, current task pointer, and the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill files.
- Confirmed the active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/01-task-remove-runner-source-cockroach-access-and-config.md`; next step is to inspect that task file and determine whether this turn must produce a plan or execute an existing plan.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is still pre-execution (`<status>not_started</status>`, not `NOW EXECUTE`), so this turn must produce a plan file and stop.
- Started codebase discovery for the runner/source split: searching for source CockroachDB access, runner config surface, and reviewing improve-code-boundaries smells around shared connection shapes and unreduced config.

- Confirmed the runner CLI still exposes `verify` and `cutover-readiness` subcommands, and `ValidatedConfig` still reports a `verify=` label, so runner still owns a source-facing contract.
- Reading runner config and error modules now to identify which config structs, parser branches, error variants, fixtures, and tests should be removed in the planning slice.

- Confirmed the main boundary problem is unreduced runner config and CLI surface: `verify` config plus `verify` / `cutover-readiness` commands keep source-database concerns inside the runner binary.
- Checking Ralph plan conventions and docs/test references now so the plan file and task-file marker use the same `TO BE VERIFIED` / `NOW EXECUTE` workflow the repo expects.

- Verified the Ralph convention by reading earlier task/plan pairs: task files carry a `<plan>...` tag, and the plan file ends with the phase marker (`TO BE VERIFIED` or `NOW EXECUTE`).
- Pulling adjacent story-16 context, README/operator docs, and CLI contract coverage now so the new plan references the existing verification/cutover work that this removal task will delete from `runner`.

- Read adjacent story-16 tasks to keep this task scoped correctly before writing the plan; I need a clean boundary between current runner-source removal work and later verify/bootstrap/README cleanup tasks.

- Story-16 sequencing still needs one explicit decision in the plan: whether task 01 removes all runner source-shaped inputs now (including `compare-schema` and `source-url` commands) or intentionally leaves anything for later tasks.
- Reading schema-compare/helper-plan code and the runner crate manifest now to resolve that scope based on actual source-boundary coupling rather than guessing.

- Doing one final architecture check before writing the plan: verify whether `runner run` actually depends on the source-shaped helper/schema/verify modules or whether they are only extra CLI surfaces that can be deleted outright.

- Wrote the story-16 planning artifact at `.ralph/tasks/story-16-runtime-split-removals/01-task-remove-runner-source-cockroach-access-and-config_plans/2026-04-19-runner-source-access-removal-plan.md`.
- Updated the active task file to point at that plan via `<plan>...</plan>`.
- Settled the execution design strongly enough to mark the plan `NOW EXECUTE`: remove all runner public source-facing surfaces (`compare-schema`, `render-helper-plan`, `verify`, `cutover-readiness`, `verify:` config), keep only destination runtime commands, and preserve helper planning only as an internal destination-owned boundary.
- Next step after quitting immediately: execute the plan via TDD slices, starting with failing CLI/config contract tests for the reduced runner surface.

- Read prior progress, the active task pointer, and the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skills on startup.
- Confirmed the current task is story 16 task 01 and previous state says its plan was already promoted to `NOW EXECUTE`.
- Reading the task file plus referenced plan now so I can execute against the exact checklist and avoid drifting from the planned boundary removal.

- Verified the plan file still ends in `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn is the implementation turn.
- Started discovery for TDD slice 1 by reading runner CLI/config/tests and searching for source-facing commands, flags, and `verify:` config references.
- Next I need to write the first failing contract around help text and validate output before deleting any runner source-facing code.

- Entered the first RED slice by tightening runner CLI and validate-config contract tests: removed source-facing command expectations and now require `verify=` to disappear from validation output.
- Running targeted runner tests now to capture the exact failures before deleting implementation.
- After the RED failures I will remove CLI subcommands and the public verify label path from the runner output layer.

- First GREEN removed the public source-only CLI commands from `lib.rs` and dropped `verify=` from validate-config output.
- Compilation immediately exposed deeper boundary coupling: `error.rs` and `helper_plan.rs` still depend on the source-schema module even after the CLI surface is gone.
- Reading those files now so I can preserve only the internal destination helper-planning boundary and continue the deletion slices cleanly.

- Slice 2 completed: the runner config schema no longer includes `verify`, fixtures were updated, and legacy `verify:` is now rejected with the parse error surfaced in stderr.
- Started the source-surface deletion slice: removed the dedicated schema/helper/verify/cutover contract tests, rewrote the README and README contract to the destination-only runner interface, and removed the public helper-plan path from `helper_plan.rs`.
- Running the narrowed runner test set now to catch remaining compile or contract fallout before pushing into the full workspace lanes.

- Runner package execution slices are now green: public source-facing CLI commands are removed, `verify:` config is gone, source-only test modules and implementations were deleted, and the README now documents only the destination runner contract plus CI publish safety.
- `cargo test -p runner` passes across bootstrap/config/CLI/README/reconcile/webhook/E2E-integrity targets after replacing verify-dependent long-lane support with destination-only assertions.
- Next I am running the required repo lanes in order: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`, then I will do the final Ralph task updates if everything stays green.

- Resumed after context compaction with the runner source-access removal changes already in place.
- Next step is to poll the active make test-long session, then either fix any remaining failures or complete task bookkeeping/commit/push.

- Polled the active make test-long session.
- The runner crate long ignored tests are passing so far; the remaining work is waiting for the overall make test-long command to finish cleanly.

- make test-long completed successfully after the runner split cleanup.
- Next step is final task bookkeeping: inspect the task file, set passes/status as appropriate, switch task, then commit and push with test evidence.

- Updated the task record to completed with passes=true and checked acceptance criteria after confirming make check/lint/test/test-long all passed.
- Next step is running Ralph task switch, then I will inspect the resulting task-tracking changes before committing and pushing.
